videos.title: USA Pickleball Referee Training: Singles Sideout Scoring
videos.description:
Learn the fundamentals of Singles Sideout Scoring and how to accurately track scoring during singles competition as a USA Pickleball referee. This training video covers score calling, serving positions, side-outs, and scorekeeping procedures used in traditional sideout scoring, where only the serving player can score points.

Designed for both new and experienced officials, this training helps ref

videos.title: USA Pickleball Referee Training: Doubles Sideout Scoring
videos.description:
Learn the fundamentals of Doubles Sideout Scoring and how to properly track scoring during a match as a USA Pickleball referee. This training video walks officials through key scoring concepts, server sequences, side-outs, and scorekeeping procedures used in traditional sideout scoring. Sideout scoring remains the official scoring format for most USA Pickleball competition.

Whether you're a new r

videos.title: Oceanview Named Title Sponsor of the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships
videos.description:
USA Pickleball is proud to announce Oceanview Life and Annuity as the new title sponsor of its premier event, now officially named the 2026 Oceanview USA Pickleball National Championships. The partnership marks a significant milestone for the organization and reinforces the continued momentum of pickleball as one of the fastest-growing sports in the country. 

Set to take place October 31–November

videos.title: What is Pickleball?
videos.description:
Pickleball is one of the fastest growing sports in the United States and for good reason. In this video, we break down what Pickleball is, why it’s so popular, and how you can get started playing.

Pickleball is a paddle sport that combines elements of tennis, table tennis, and badminton into one fun and accessible game. One of its biggest appeals is that players of all ages and skill levels can p

items.title: How to Stop Secretly Blaming Your Pickleball Partner
items.summary: Blaming your pickleball partner rarely helps the next point. Instead, turn mistakes into useful information: use calm body language, give one clear cue, avoid mid-game lectures, and focus on your own next action. Better partners don’t ignore errors — they respond in ways that kee
items.author: Ana

items.title: Three Small Pickleball Habits That Make You Look Way Smarter on Court
items.summary: To play smarter pickleball, move with the direction of your shot, read both your ball and your partner’s, and stop overhitting. For many rec players, swinging at 65–70% with better placement creates more control, fewer errors, and better court positioning than trying to overpower
items.author: Ana

items.title: Most Pickleball Mistakes Come From the Transition Zone: This Drill Can Help
items.summary: Mari Humberg breaks down her complete transition zone drill sequence, featuring five essential drills that will transform how you move from baseline to kitchen. Master the transition zone drill and become a more complete pickleball player.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The 5 Mistakes 4.0 Pickleball Players Make Over and Over
items.summary: Most 4.0 pickleball players already have the shots. The real problem is pickleball decision making, pressure management, and knowing when to use the right tool.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Demystifying the Third Shot Drop: 3 Simple Tips from a Pro
items.summary: A pro pickleball player reveals the three most common mistakes that are sabotaging your third shot drop. Here's what you need to fix to improve consistency and control.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 3 Skills That Separate 4.0 From 5.0 Pickleball Players
items.summary: Professional pickleball player Ashley Griffith breaks down the exact 5.0 pickleball skills that separate elite competitors from 4.0 players. These aren't flashy shots—they're fundamentals that win matches.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 3 Ways to Stop Getting Attacked at the Kitchen in Pickleball
items.summary: Getting attacked at the kitchen isn't bad luck—it's because you're giving your opponents attackable balls. Learn the three mistakes that lead to kitchen attacks and the drills that will transform your game.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Technical Fix to Stop Popping Up Your Pickleball Shot
items.summary: One of the most common mistakes in pickleball is the pop-up shot, which gives your opponent an easy put-away opportunity. Here's how to eliminate this costly error from every part of the court.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Pickleball Pattern Drill: How to Train Like a Pro
items.summary: The pickleball pattern drill is one of the most effective ways to sharpen your counters and anticipation. Cori Elliott breaks down exactly how to run this drill and why it matters for competitive play.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Punch, Flick & Roll: A Complete Guide to the Backhand Volley in Pickleball
items.summary: The backhand volley is actually three distinct shots, each with different setups, mechanics, and strategic applications
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: 12 Pickleball Tips That Will Transform Your Game
items.summary: Two experienced pickleball players break down 12 essential pickleball tips that will transform how you approach the sport. From injury prevention to advanced footwork, these insights can help you avoid costly mistakes and accelerate your improvement.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: The Complete Guide to Left Side Dinking in Pickleball
items.summary: Left side dinking isn't just about taking more balls—it's about positioning, footwork, and court control.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: MLP Columbus Event Recap - The NJ 5s Get Revenge
items.summary: This is an event recap of the 2026 MLP Columbus event, held at Pickle & Chill in Columbus, Ohio. The New Jersey Fives came out victorious, earning themselves 25 regular season standings points.
items.author: Erik Tice

items.title: How to Hold a Pickleball Paddle for Beginners: Grip Basics That Change Everything
items.summary: Knowing how to hold a pickleball paddle for beginners is the single most important skill you can build before touching the court. Get your grip wrong and everything else, your dinks, your drives, your serves, collapses with it.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: Need a New Pickleball Paddle? These Databases Can Help
items.summary: It’s getting pretty confusing out there. But there’s good news: it’s never been a better time to be a paddle consumer. Get the facts before you buy.
items.author: Alex E. Weaver

items.title: The Backhand Slice Dink Detail Most Rec Players Miss
items.summary: The backhand slice dink is one of those shots that looks simple until you actually try to control it. You want the ball to stay low. You want a little bite. You want it to land softly in the kitchen and make your opponent lift the next ball. Instead, it dives into the net.Or it [
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Use the Transition Zone to Attack Before Reaching the Kitchen
items.summary: Knowing how to attack from transition zone pickleball situations is one of the fastest ways to upgrade your offensive game. This guide breaks down exactly when to pull the trigger, which shots to use, and how to stop bleeding points in no-man's land.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Win More Points Off Your Pickleball Serve: Placement and Spin Combos
items.summary: Winning points off your pickleball serve starts with understanding placement and spin, two tools most players massively underuse. This guide breaks down the exact combos that force weak returns and set you up to finish the point.
items.author: The Dink Media Team

items.title: How to Use a Dink Fake-Out in Doubles
items.summary: A dink fake-out works by making your setup look like a normal soft dink, then changing the finish with a subtle open paddle face and slice across the ball. Use it during crosscourt dink rallies when opponents start leaning early, so the ball spins away and forces a late reach. Ev
items.author: Ana

items.title: Why Your Knee Hurts on the Inside After Pickleball
items.summary: Inner knee pain after pickleball can come from MCL stress, especially after quick cuts, wide lunges, planted-foot pivots, or sudden side-to-side stops. Unlike outside knee pain, it often points to medial knee overload and needs different prevention: better knee tracking, hip stre
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Fastest Way From 4.0 to 5.0+ Is Not More Power — It’s Learning How to Stop Losing the Point
items.summary: To reach 5.0 pickleball faster, stop relying only on power. Train resets, pace control, fifth-shot patterns, transition defense, and smarter attack decisions. Open play alone usually is not enough; combine drilling, competitive games, video review, and targeted practice against s
items.author: Ana

items.title: How to Punish Players Who Sit Backhand at the Kitchen
items.summary: To beat players who sit backhand, stop feeding their paddle with hard, flat speedups. Use a slower, heavier roll into the shoulder or upper-body zone. That higher ball can jam their backhand counter, force awkward contact, and create weak replies or pop-ups for the next shot. Som
items.author: Ana

items.title: Should You Target Women in Pickleball? The Honest Rec-Player Etiquette Guide
items.summary: Targeting a weaker player in pickleball is legal strategy, but targeting someone simply because she is a woman is lazy and often rude. In competitive play, target skill gaps. In social rec play, keep the game respectful, safe, and fun: play the player, not the stereotype. There a
items.author: Ana

items.title: The Swerve Serve in Pickleball: Smart Weapon or Consistency Trap?
items.summary: A pickleball swerve serve uses sidespin to make the ball bend or kick sideways, often pulling the returner off balance. It can be legal if the spin comes from paddle contact, not the release, but intermediate players should use it as a changeup—not a replacement for a reliable de
items.author: Ana

items.title: Clear Skies Hospitality Acquires The Exchange, New Orleans’ Dedicated Pickleball Hub [VIDEO]
items.summary: New Orleans pickleball just got a bigger connection to one of Chicago’s fastest-growing pickleball lifestyle brands. Clear Skies Hospitality, the company behind Chicago’s SPF pickleball clubs, has acquired The Exchange, New Orleans’ first and only dedicated pickleball facility. T
items.author: Ana

items.title: Be a Tiger, Not a Cheetah: The Art of Speed Control
items.summary: One of the earliest lessons most pickleball players learn is simple: Get to the non-volley zone as fast as you can. While that advice isn’t wrong, it’s incomplete—and for many players it becomes a ceiling rather than a foundation. Winning points at higher levels isn’t about how f
items.author: Suzee Anderson

items.title: The Pickleball Puzzle: How to Outsmart, Outlast, and Outplay Every Opponent
items.summary: In pickleball, every game is a puzzle. Each point is another clue. The best players don’t just react, they solve. Whether you’re facing bangers or defensive grinders, your job is not simply to hit good shots. Your job is to decode and execute your plan against the equation in fro
items.author: Kyle McMakin

items.title: Back to Baseline: A Winning One-Day Meal Plan
items.summary: You may have watched the after-match interview of an adorable young pro player who openly admitted to having an “awful” diet. It caused chuckles across our pickle-munity to hear that this top athlete is flourishing despite his not-so-golden health habit. I think we all appreciate
items.author: Brandi Givens

items.title: LevelUp Pickleball Club Opens in Pittsburgh
items.summary: Levelup Pickleball Club Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania In the south suburbs of Pittsburgh, where football has normally dominated the sports landscape, pickleball has surged in popularity. Where there were once just about 200 avid pickleballers (eight years ago), that number is now clos
items.author: Drew Wathey

items.title: Rachel Rohrabacher
items.summary: Hometown: Tampa, FL Age: 28 Rating: 6.13 Favorite Event: PPA Cincinnati You were an outstanding doubles player in tennis at the University of South Carolina. What of those skills carried over into pickleball? Anticipation at the net and reaction time translated immediately, along
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Caribbean Capital of Pickleball
items.summary: With 71 courts across eight islands, Sandals and Beaches Resorts are rapidly becoming some of the world’s most exciting destinations for pickleball enthusiasts. Sandals South Coast In the world of luxury travel, the Caribbean has long been known as the capital of sun and sand, bu
items.author: Julie Talerico

items.title: Pickleball is the Destination
items.summary: Aurora Anguilla Resort These luxury getaways prove pickleball can be as refined as it is fun. As pickleball continues its global rise, select resorts are elevating the sport from a casual amenity to a defining pillar of the guest experience. In each issue of Pickleball Magazine,
items.author: Laura Gainor

items.title: Why Beginners Should Take Lessons
items.summary: When I was 5 years old, my mother took me for my first swim lesson. The instructor, a gruff man in red lifeguard Speedos, led me to the deep end of the pool and pushed me in. With my mother looking on in shock, I managed to dog-paddle to the side safely. At which point, the instr
items.author: Rick Cipes

items.title: SINGLES STYLES AND HOW TO PLAY AGAINST THEM
items.summary: You are better off going into a match with a plan. You need a baseline to be able to change a plan if it is not working. Good players will adjust, so you must be able to adjust as well. You certainly need to know yourself, what you can and cannot do. Good players have a weapon, a
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Wild Dunes Resort Pickleball Giveaway official rules
items.summary: OFFICIAL RULES Pickleball Magazine x Wild Dunes Resort x Pickleball in the Sun Sweepstakes NO PURCHASE NECESSARY TO ENTER OR WIN. A PURCHASE OR PAYMENT OF ANY KIND WILL NOT INCREASE YOUR CHANCES OF WINNING. VOID WHERE PROHIBITED OR RESTRICTED BY LAW. 1. Sponsor & Administrator Th
items.author: Laura Gainor

items.title: WHY SPORTSMANSHIP MATTERS: THE OBVIOUS AND NOT-SO-OBVIOUS REASONS
items.summary: We all want our sport to be respected. We don’t want to bring shame to pickleball as it develops into a recognized paddle sport. To honor the founding principles of the game and positively guide this development, we must work to exhibit good sportsmanship at all levels. Not only
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: 7 Core Shots Every 4.0+ Player Must Master
items.summary: Elevating your game beyond intermediate isn’t about flashy winners—it’s about mastering essential concepts that improve consistency, control, and point construction. These seven fundamental shots separate a 3.5 from a dependable 4.0+ player, and practicing them with purpose makes
items.author: Kyle McMakin

items.title: Is Your Game One of Substance, or is it Reactionary?
items.summary: Becoming a better player involves the ability to dictate play. Most pickleball players begin by being reactionary: “Just get the return of serve back into the court.” “Keep the ball in play.” “Just get the ball in to start play—the serve doesn’t matter.” If your play is in line w
items.author: Alice Tym

items.title: Making Pickleball Fun for Kids!
items.summary: So what’s the secret for ensuring that your children or grandchildren have a positive first-time experience with pickleball, encouraging them to make it a lifetime activity they can love as much as we all do? First, remember your experience learning the game. Was it a friend or f
items.author: Jason Jamison

items.title: VARIATIONS ON THE BACKHAND SERVE
items.summary: As pickleball attracts more and more players from other sports, those new players bring shots from their former sports. There is much greater shot variety in pickleball than ever before. Some of the shots even have names, like reset, Erne, and dink. As overall play gets better, t
items.author: Alice Tym
